title: Note as Letter to Self
html:
A persistent Yahir writing form: second-person, present-tense, addressed to himself as a way to silence or process feelings before they take hold. The earliest clean specimen is the 2019 Phoenix→Portland flight letter (2019 09 10 low key writing this to end) — written the night before school starts to "end, more like slightly silence my thoughts or feeling for the girl you meet, Alexandria Nagy." The structural template is already complete: name the feeling, acknowledge the person, then explicitly order the self to march on ("Remember Alexandria, because she was special, but don't get trapped or glued or even stuck on her, advance in this life and world").

The form recurs across the corpus. The 2024 11 28 leading thoughts about 6 month adventure manifesto (16 days before leaving China) uses the same address — "Love like your soul is unraveling," "When shit goes south, stay calm, tap into inner error correcting voice, fitfo" — bullet-form commands to a future self. The 2024 12 14 departing china in-flight reflection sustains it: "Be so truly yourself it catches people off guard. Honest, genuine, and Yahir Gonzalez." By 2024 05 13 things it shows up as repeated mantras across date headers — "feel your emotions, do not block them off" and the "write yourself a letter 1 year from now, 3 years from now…" prompt — both lines appearing verbatim multiple times, a deliberate self-injection.

The most stylistically interesting case is 2025 03 01 peacock bar incident: Yahir writes the night-of in the raw second-person processing mode, then on a later pass rewrites the same event in clean third-person, lawyer-ready prose. The two passes side-by-side make the form's purpose explicit — second-person is for moving through an event, third-person is for recording it. The April 2025 biometrics summary suggests an even further extension of the pattern: the survey paper's framing of credentials as surrogates vs bindings maps onto a note as a surrogate for memory rather than the memory itself.

This is one of Yahir's stylistic fingerprints — alongside the stacked column note form, it's how the most personal entries in the corpus identify themselves at a glance.

Source summaries

  • 2019 09 10 low key writing this to end — earliest clean specimen
  • 2024 05 13 things — repeated injunctions form
  • 2024 11 28 leading thoughts about 6 month adventure — manifesto form
  • 2024 12 14 departing china — in-flight identity statement
  • 2025 03 01 peacock bar incident — first-person + third-person rewrite pair
  • 2025 04 27 screenshot 2025 04 27 at 7 41 04 pm 5f2b64af — surrogate/binding analogue

Connections

  • fear of not doing — the engine driving the addresses
  • legacy question — the recurring topic
  • feel your emotions — a pinned injunction
  • letter to future self — explicit variant
  • end of chapter reflection — when the form deploys
  • stacked column note — sibling stylistic form
  • fitfo — the operational rule it carries
